text:
Once upon a time, in a lush, verdant forest, there was a small but mighty tree named Maple. Maple was special because it had a heart full of love and a spirit that could touch the lives of all who came near.

Every morning, as the sun peeked through the canopy and painted the ground in warm hues, Maple would wave its branches gently to the breeze. This simple gesture brought comfort to a young qilin (a mythical creature resembling a horse with antlers and a dragon's head) who lived nearby. The qilin, named Luna, would come out from her hiding spot under a large oak to bask in the warmth of Maple’s gentle greeting.

One day, a fierce storm rolled into the forest, its winds strong and its rain relentless. Trees groaned and branches snapped, and the forest seemed to hold its breath in anticipation of the coming destruction. Maple, however, stood tall and strong, its roots digging deep into the soil to anchor it firmly.
